description: Best practices for using AI-Hydro tools effectively and correctly

practices.title: Always Read Tool Documentation First
workflow: 1. Identify the tool you need to use
practices.rationale: Pre-reading documentation prevents 90% of field name errors and hallucinations

practices.title: Use Exact Field Names from Documentation
steps: Do not type them from memory
Do not assume based on external API knowledge

example.incorrect: site_id = result['site_no']  # Assumed from NWIS knowledge

practices.title: Handle Missing/Optional Fields Gracefully

patterns.try_except:
try: value = result['field_name']
except KeyError: value = None

practices.when_to_use: When tool documentation indicates a field might not always be present

practices.title: Follow CAMELS Naming Conventions
practices.benefit: Consistent naming makes code self-documenting and maintainable

practices.title: Validate Input Parameters
practices.example:
if not re.match(r'^\d{8}$', gauge_id):
    raise ValueError('Invalid gauge ID format')

practices.title: Document Data Sources and Transformations
practices.example:
# Streamflow from USGS NWIS (01031500), 2010-2020
# Units: cubic meters per second (m³/s)
# Transformed from cfs using drainage area

practices.title: Provide Clear Error Messages
practices.example:
KeyError: 'site_no' not found.
Use 'gauge_id' instead (see tool documentation).
Available fields: gauge_id, gauge_name, gauge_lat, gauge_lon

readability: Use descriptive variable names matching field names
maintainability: Define field name constants for frequently used fields
Create helper functions for common access patterns
reproducibility: Include tool version numbers in comments

testing_recommendations: Test with multiple gauge IDs to verify generality
Test edge cases (extreme dates, coordinates, etc.)
